atomicity.message: The staged diff is exactly one feature: lint Unit C safe manifest writer and recommendations file, limited to internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go and internal/backpressure/lint_writer_test.go.

conditions.message: The staged Go writer code is formatted and passes the relevant lint-writer tests and full staged-index suite.

git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go | rg found shared helpers validateLintCommandProposals, applyLintCommandProposals, applyLintCoverageUpdate, lintCommandNode, replaceLintRecommendationsSection, lintRecommendationsSection, atomicWriteFile, and fileModeOrDefault.
git diff --cached --check passed.

verifier.evidence_ref: bead scope; staged writer/test inspection; focused tests

conditions.message: The staged payload does not claim fake lint enforcement. It writes explicit lint_commands only from proposals, records skipped/updated/renamed outcomes, and keeps structural rules as recommendations prose under a marked section.
evidence: br show project-readiness-kit-lint-unit-c-manifest-writer-3kb --json states structural rules are recommendations only unless backed by a real command/analyzer, and project scripts/configs/CI/dependencies must not be silently edited.
git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go showed lintRecommendationsSection writes recommendation bullets and a no-recommendations fallback, not enforcement entries.
git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer_test.go showed TestLintRulesRecommendationsReplacementIsIdempotent asserting structural rules remain prose until a user enables an executable analyzer.
go test ./cmd/burpvalve ./internal/backpressure -run 'Test.*ManifestWriter|Test.*LintRules|Test.*Idempotent|Test.*Conflict' -count=1 passed.

conditions.message: The new writer behavior has direct tests for manifest preservation, idempotency, duplicate rejection, conflict handling, recommendation replacement, and atomic-failure cleanup.

Tests assert content preservation, changed metadata, conflict skip/update/rename behavior, marked-section replacement, and no leftover .tmp-* file on failure.

verifier.evidence_ref: mutating writer code and atomic failure tests

conditions.message: This condition is governed because the helper mutates manifest/recommendation files, and the staged implementation contains the expected safeguards: plan-first behavior, temp+rename writes, mode preservation, duplicate/conflict validation before mutation, and failure cleanup tests.
evidence: WriteLintManifestUpdate calls PlanLintManifestUpdate first and returns without writing when err != nil or result.Changed is false.
WriteLintRulesRecommendationsUpdate creates the backpressure directory only for the recommendations file, then writes through atomicWriteFile.
atomicWriteFile uses os.CreateTemp in the target directory, writes/chmods/closes, then os.Rename to replace the target; deferred cleanup removes the temp file unless rename succeeds.
validateLintCommandProposals rejects duplicate proposal IDs, missing command/paths, invalid paths/run_directory, non-positive timeout_seconds, and unsupported on_conflict before apply/write.
validateExistingLintCommandIDs rejects duplicate existing lint command IDs before mutation.
TestLintManifestWriterDoesNotLeavePartialManifestOnAtomicFailure simulates failure and asserts no .tmp-* residue remains.

conditions.message: The YAML/data mutation surface preserves existing manifest content where yaml.v3 allows, validates duplicate and malformed proposals before writing, normalizes repo-relative paths, returns before/after content, and makes reruns idempotent.
evidence: git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go showed LintFileUpdateResult carries Path, Before, After, Changed, Added, Updated, Renamed, and Skipped metadata for preview/audit.

git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go showed validateLintCommandProposals rejects duplicate proposal IDs, missing id/command/paths, invalid paths/run_directory, non-positive timeouts, and unsupported on_conflict; validateExistingLintCommandIDs rejects duplicate existing IDs.

rg -n 'func normalizeRepoRelativePath|\.\.' internal/backpressure internal/lintconfig showed the existing normalizeRepoRelativePath rejects '..' and '../' paths; the staged writer calls it for paths, run_directory, and lint_coverage.declined_roots.

git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer_test.go showed tests for preserving comments/order/content, idempotent zero-diff rerun, duplicate proposal/existing IDs before write, skip/update/rename conflicts, unknown conflict rejection, and recommendation replacement.

verifier.evidence_ref: file I/O inspection; no process/script/CI edit grep; full tests

conditions.message: The governed file-write surface was inspected. The writer only reads/writes repo lint policy files, uses repo-relative path validation, does not execute commands or edit scripts/CI/dependencies, and preserves modes through temp+rename writes.
evidence: git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go | rg showed file I/O limited to os.ReadFile, os.MkdirAll for the lint-rules directory, os.CreateTemp, temp.Write, temp.Chmod, os.Rename, os.Remove temp cleanup, and os.Stat for existing mode.
git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go | rg for exec/Command/package.json/workflow/CI showed no process execution, project script editing, CI editing, or dependency-install behavior.

git show :internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go showed atomicWriteFile writes in the target directory to a dot-temp file, chmods to preserved/default mode, closes, then renames into place, removing temp on failure.
go test ./... passed.

verifier.evidence_ref: staged file list and condition text inspection

conditions.message: The staged payload has no governed visual surface: it adds backend manifest/recommendation writer code and tests, not UI rendering, screenshots, responsive layout, canvas, or visual-diff behavior.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status lists only internal/backpressure/lint_writer.go and internal/backpressure/lint_writer_test.go as new files.
backpressure/visual-regression.md governs screenshot, browser, responsive layout, canvas, and visual-diff checks; none of those surfaces are changed by this staged payload.

verifier.evidence_ref: writer algorithm inspection and staged-index tests

conditions.message: The writer performs bounded YAML parsing and linear scans over proposals/existing lint command nodes; it does not introduce recursive repo scans, command execution, network work, or concurrency fanout.
evidence: applyLintManifestUpdate unmarshals one manifest YAML document, validates proposal IDs in one map pass, builds an existing command ID index, and applies proposals over the provided slice.
replaceLintRecommendationsSection uses bounded string Index/Trim operations against one lint-rules document and replaces a marked section instead of appending duplicates.
Staged diff grep found no WalkDir, goroutine, exec.Command, network URL, or external process execution additions in lint_writer.go.
Clean staged-index snapshot /tmp/lint-c-index.pppvPL passed go test ./....

verifier.evidence_ref: plan/write API separation and conflict behavior inspection

conditions.message: The staged API preserves an approval boundary: callers can preview before/after content and changed metadata before invoking the write path, conflicts default to skip, and structural lint recommendations remain prose rather than hidden enforcement.
evidence: PlanLintManifestUpdate and PlanLintRulesRecommendationsUpdate return Before, After, Changed, and path metadata without writing files.
WriteLintManifestUpdate only writes when the planned result changed and no error occurred; default on_conflict behavior is skip, with update/rename requiring explicit action fields.

LintRules recommendations are placed in a marked section and the test fixture explicitly preserves project-owned guidance while adding prose stating structural rules remain prose until a user enables an executable analyzer.
No staged code edits project scripts, dependencies, CI, hooks, or runs generated lint commands.
